en.extract:
Jameson's wattle-eye (Platysteira jamesoni) is a species of bird in the family Platysteiridae. It is found in Democratic Republic of the Congo, Kenya, South Sudan, Tanzania, and Uganda. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. It is named after James Sligo Jameson.

'''Jameson's wattle-eye''' ('''''Platysteira jamesoni''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Platysteiridae]]. It is found in [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]], [[Kenya]], [[South Sudan]], [[Tanzania]], and [[Uganda]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. It is named after [[James Sligo Jameson]].<ref>{{cite book | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=g_3QDwAAQBAJ&dq=James+Sligo+Jameson+antpecker&pg=PA283 | title=The Eponym Dictionary of Birds | publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing |last1=Beolens |first1=Bo |last2=Watkins |first2=Michael |last3=Grayson |first3=Michael | year=2020 | page=283}}</ref>
